baseline.source: RCH ts1 profile recorded in bead frankenjax-jhgap before the lever

candidate.source: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline random/uniform_1m_f64 -- --quiet

score.reason: candidate midpoint was 2.50x slower than the ts1 baseline; even as a cross-worker result this is a clear regression, not a keepable win

lever_tested: Partition the independent ThreeFry counter range into scoped-thread contiguous chunks for large random_uniform counts.

isomorphism.rng_order: Each element i used the same ThreeFry key and counter [0, i] as the scalar path.
isomorphism.floating_point: The candidate used the same 23-bit mantissa extraction and minval + unit * scale formula per element.
isomorphism.tie_breaking: No ordering or tie-breaking surface changed.
isomorphism.result: Behavior proof passed, but the lever was not retained because performance regressed.

proof.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib threefry -- --nocapture

decision: Rejected and source restored; git diff for crates/fj-lax/src/threefry.rs is empty.
next_primitive: Attack a fused ThreeFry-to-normal stream / distribution-specific generator that eliminates intermediate uniform allocation and repeated scalar transform overhead, target >=1.4x on random/normal_1m_f64.
